top_findings.title: Brasileirão loader reads away team from the away_team_state column
top_findings.evidence: loader.go:48 sets AwayTeam from row[4]; header is datetime,home_team,home_team_state,away_team(3),away_team_state(4),... so row[4] is the 2-letter state. Sample: 'Palmeiras-SP,SP,Portuguesa-SP,SP,1,1' -> AwayTeam becomes 'SP'. All 4,180 Brasileirão away teams are corrupted to state codes, breaking away-team search, head-to-head, standings and team stats for the flagship dataset.
top_findings.suggestion: Read AwayTeam from row[3] (away_team), not row[4].

top_findings.title: TeamStats win/draw/loss classification is case-sensitive but lookup is case-insensitive
top_findings.evidence: store.go:84 finds matches via lowercase index key, but store.go:101-107 compares m.HomeTeam == team / m.AwayTeam == team case-sensitively. A caller passing 'flamengo' (any non-exact case) still gets Matches>0 from the index, yet every match falls through to the else branch (store.go:110) and is counted as a Loss, so Wins=Draws=0 and WinRate=0. Only exact-case inputs (as in the tests) are correct.
top_findings.suggestion: Use strings.EqualFold(m.HomeTeam, team) / EqualFold(m.AwayTeam, team) for the W/D/L branches, consistent with the case-insensitive index.
